label: Gaza City
sub: Capital of the Gaza Strip and main urban battleground. IDF ground forces encircled and entered the city from late October 2023, fighting house-to-house in dense neighborhoods including Shejaiya and Jabalia.

label: Jabalia Camp
sub: Gaza's largest refugee camp, repeatedly struck by Israeli airstrikes. An Oct 31, 2023 strike killed at least 126 people; subsequent strikes in November 2023 killed dozens more. One of the most heavily bombed areas of the war.

label: Al-Shifa Hospital
sub: Gaza's largest medical complex, raided by IDF in November 2023 and again in March 2024. Israel claimed it housed Hamas command center; WHO and MSF reported the hospital was rendered non-functional. Major focus of international controversy.

label: Rafah
sub: Southernmost city in Gaza; became refuge for over 1.4 million displaced Palestinians before IDF launched ground operation in May 2024. Site of major hostage rescue operations and continued fighting through 2025.

label: Rafah Crossing
sub: Gaza's main border crossing with Egypt and primary entry point for humanitarian aid. Seized by IDF in May 2024; remained closed for extended periods, drastically reducing aid flows. Critical bottleneck for the humanitarian response.

label: Kerem Shalom Crossing
sub: Primary commercial crossing between Israel and Gaza. Main entry point for food and supplies. Hamas rocket strikes killed IDF soldiers at the crossing in June 2024 prompting Israel to temporarily close it. Key chokepoint for aid.

label: Khan Younis
sub: Gaza's second-largest city; major IDF ground operation from January 2024. Believed to be location of senior Hamas leadership including Yahya Sinwar. Heavily damaged; Nasser Hospital compound used as field headquarters by IDF.

label: Nasser Hospital
sub: Khan Younis's main hospital; IDF surrounded and raided in February 2024, forcing evacuation of patients. Hospital lost power and medical supplies during siege. WHO declared it non-functional after raid.

label: Deir al-Balah
sub: Central Gaza district that became main hub for displaced Palestinians and humanitarian operations. Site of World Central Kitchen convoy strike (Apr 1, 2024) killing 7 aid workers. Location of major UN humanitarian compound.

label: Al-Mawasi Humanitarian Zone
sub: Strip of coastal land designated by IDF as a humanitarian safe zone for displaced Palestinians. Over 1 million people sheltered there in tent camps. Repeatedly struck despite designation; May 2024 strike killed 45 people.

label: Beit Lahiya
sub: Northern Gaza town severely damaged in the initial ground offensive. IDF forces used the area as a staging ground for the push into Gaza City. Large civilian evacuation warnings issued. Much of the town destroyed.

label: Beit Hanoun
sub: Northeastern Gaza town adjacent to Israeli territory, one of the first areas targeted in the ground offensive. Heavily bombed in initial stages; the town was almost entirely destroyed and depopulated.

label: Shejaiya
sub: Eastern neighborhood of Gaza City and site of particularly intense urban combat. IDF fought Hamas in dense building-to-building fighting over multiple months. Major tunnel network discovered. Largely destroyed.

label: Tel al-Hawa
sub: Southwestern Gaza City neighborhood with high concentration of residential buildings. Heavily bombed; location of several UN-sheltering schools struck during fighting.

label: Nuseirat Camp
sub: Central Gaza refugee camp; site of June 8, 2024 IDF hostage rescue operation that killed over 274 Palestinians and freed 4 hostages. One of the deadliest single-day incidents of the war.

label: Bureij Camp
sub: Central Gaza refugee camp repeatedly struck in airstrikes. Overcrowded with displaced persons from northern Gaza. Infrastructure largely destroyed.

label: Maghazi Camp
sub: Central Gaza refugee camp; site of December 24, 2023 airstrike on residential buildings that killed over 70 people on Christmas Eve. Multiple subsequent strikes throughout the conflict.

label: Al-Ahli Arab Hospital
sub: Anglican-run hospital in Gaza City; site of October 17, 2023 explosion that killed hundreds. Hamas blamed Israel; Israel and US said misfired Palestinian rocket caused blast. One of most disputed incidents of the war.

label: Kerem Abu Salem (Kerem Shalom)
sub: Israel's main truck crossing into Gaza for humanitarian aid. Repeatedly contested crossing point for aid delivery. Key location for ceasefire negotiations focused on humanitarian access.

label: Erez Crossing
sub: Northern pedestrian crossing between Israel and Gaza. Attacked by Hamas on October 7 and closed since. Was the primary crossing for Palestinian workers entering Israel before the war.

label: Kibbutz Be'eri
sub: Israeli agricultural community attacked on October 7; 108 of ~1,100 residents killed — one of the highest per-capita death tolls of any community. Over 30 taken hostage. Community largely destroyed.

label: Kibbutz Kfar Aza
sub: Israeli kibbutz devastated in Hamas October 7 attack. Over 63 residents killed and dozens taken hostage. Soldiers described scenes of mass atrocities. Community evacuated and partially destroyed.

label: Nova Festival Site
sub: Site of the Supernova Sukkot Gathering music festival attacked by Hamas on October 7; 364 young people killed in the single deadliest location of the October 7 attack. Became memorial site.

label: Kibbutz Nirim
sub: Small Israeli kibbutz attacked on October 7; multiple residents killed, some taken hostage. Community evacuated and relocated. Part of the Gaza envelope communities devastated in the attack.

label: Sderot
sub: Israeli city near Gaza border; attacked on October 7 with gunmen entering the city. Police station targeted; civilians killed. Thousands evacuated as rocket attacks continue throughout the war. Israel's 'missile city' facing constant rocket fire.

label: Tal al-Sultan (Rafah)
sub: Densely packed displacement area in western Rafah. Site of May 26, 2024 Israeli airstrike that ignited tent fires killing 45 displaced Palestinians. Previously designated as humanitarian safe zone.

label: Salah al-Din Road (Netzarim Corridor)
sub: Main north-south road of Gaza; IDF established the Netzarim Corridor cutting Gaza into northern and southern halves, making movement extremely difficult for civilians. Used as main evacuation route under military conditions.

label: Netzarim Corridor
sub: IDF-established military corridor bisecting Gaza east-to-west along what was the former Israeli settlement of Netzarim. Effectively divided Gaza into north and south zones, controlling civilian movement. Maintained throughout ground operations.

label: Philadelphi Corridor
sub: Narrow strip of land along the Gaza-Egypt border. Israel insists on military control of corridor to prevent weapons smuggling via tunnels; point of major contention in ceasefire negotiations. Hamas demanded full Israeli withdrawal; Israel refused.

label: Khan Younis Displacement Camp
sub: Massive informal displacement camp south of Khan Younis hosting hundreds of thousands of internally displaced Palestinians. Tents and makeshift shelters stretched for kilometers. Limited sanitation, medical care, and food.

label: Al-Zawayda
sub: Central Gaza town that became major transit point for displaced Palestinians moving between north and south Gaza. Location of humanitarian aid distribution points.

label: Beach (Shati) Camp
sub: Coastal refugee camp in Gaza City; heavily struck in the air campaign. Dense population made evacuation difficult. Sustained severe damage throughout the campaign against northern Gaza.

label: Al-Quds Hospital
sub: PRCS-operated hospital in Gaza City that struggled to function without fuel, power, or supplies. Repeatedly at center of warnings about hospital closures. Partially operational during some periods.

label: Jordanian Field Hospital
sub: Field hospital established by Jordan near Khan Younis to treat wounded Palestinians. One of several Arab-nation field hospitals deployed in Gaza. Critical backup for overwhelmed permanent health facilities.

label: European Hospital
sub: Major medical facility in Khan Younis; became one of the most important functioning hospitals in southern Gaza. Treated thousands of war wounded. Struggled with power, supplies, and overcrowding throughout the war.

label: Kamal Adwan Hospital
sub: Main hospital in northern Beit Lahiya; repeatedly besieged and struck. IDF raided in December 2024, arrested director Dr. Hussam Abu Safiya. Became symbol of healthcare system collapse in northern Gaza.

label: Erez Industrial Zone
sub: Former joint Israeli-Palestinian industrial zone on Gaza's northern border. Seized by Hamas on October 7. Israeli forces subsequently regained control. Area heavily bombed.

label: Gaza Perimeter Fence
sub: Israel's multi-layer security barrier that Hamas breached at approximately 30 points on October 7, 2023. The fence system, which cost approximately $1 billion to build, was overwhelmed by coordinated attacks using paragliders, bulldozers, and motorcycles.

label: Sinwar Elimination Site (Rafah)
sub: Location in Tal al-Sultan area of Rafah where Hamas supreme leader Yahya Sinwar was killed on October 16, 2024 during an IDF infantry patrol encounter. Identified posthumously via DNA and fingerprints.

label: US Temporary Floating Pier (JLOTS)
sub: US military temporary floating pier constructed off Gaza coast in May 2024 to deliver humanitarian aid by sea. Repeatedly damaged by rough weather and had to be towed to Israel for repairs. Delivered very limited quantities of aid before being dismantled.

label: UNRWA HQ Gaza
sub: UNRWA headquarters and primary coordination center for UN relief operations in Gaza. Surrounded by fighting throughout the conflict. Israel alleged Hamas maintained tunnels under UNRWA premises; UNRWA disputed these claims.

label: Tuffah (Khan Younis)
sub: Neighborhood in Khan Younis where some of the most intense urban combat took place during the January–April 2024 IDF operation. Hamas fighters used tunnel networks extensively for ambushes and resupply.

label: World Central Kitchen Strike Site
sub: Location where IDF missiles struck three World Central Kitchen aid vehicles on April 1, 2024, killing 7 aid workers in clearly marked humanitarian convoy. Strike triggered global outrage. IDF fired two officers after investigation.

label: Kuwait Roundabout (Flour Massacre)
sub: Location near the Kuwait Roundabout in Gaza City where at least 112 Palestinians were killed on February 29, 2024 while waiting for aid trucks. Disputed: Israel says crowd stampede, Palestinians say soldiers opened fire.

label: UNRWA Jabalia School Shelters
sub: Multiple UNRWA school buildings in Jabalia used as shelters for displaced Palestinians. Several struck in Israeli airstrikes, including strikes in May and June 2024. UN condemned each strike as a violation of international law.

label: Al-Rashid Street (Coast Road)
sub: Main coastal road running along Gaza's western coastline. Designated as humanitarian evacuation route at various points. Flooded with displaced Palestinians moving south. Scenes of mass civilian displacement throughout the war.

label: Zikim (IDF Staging Area)
sub: Area north of Gaza used by IDF as staging ground for northern Gaza ground operations. IDF troops assembled and launched incursions from border areas near Zikim and Yad Mordechai.

label: Kerem Shalom Rocket Strike (Jun 2024)
sub: Hamas rocket attack on Kerem Shalom crossing on June 1, 2024 killed four Israeli soldiers and damaged aid trucks waiting to enter Gaza. Israel temporarily closed the crossing in response, halting humanitarian aid.

label: Re'im Military Base
sub: Israeli military base near the Nova festival site attacked on October 7 by Hamas. The base was briefly overrun before IDF forces repelled attackers. Many festival-goers sought refuge near the base.

label: Netivot
sub: Israeli city struck by Hamas rockets throughout the war. Located east of Gaza. Missile attacks caused evacuations and civilian anxiety though Iron Dome intercepted most rockets.

label: Ashkelon
sub: Israeli coastal city north of Gaza repeatedly targeted by Hamas rockets. Multiple direct hits on residential buildings despite Iron Dome. Thousands of residents evacuated. Hospital basement converted to emergency facility.

label: Ben Gurion International Airport
sub: Israel's main international airport. Several long-range rockets from Gaza and Hezbollah missiles targeted the airport area during the conflict. Multiple airlines suspended Israel flights during periods of escalation.

label: Jerusalem / Al-Quds
sub: Holy city and flashpoint for the Israel-Palestine conflict. Al-Aqsa Mosque compound remains center of tension. Protests and clashes erupted in East Jerusalem after October 7. Capital claimed by both Israel and Palestinians.

label: Jenin Refugee Camp (West Bank)
sub: Major refugee camp in the West Bank; site of large-scale IDF Operation Iron Wall in January 2025. Weeks-long operation killed dozens, displaced 40,000+ Palestinians, and left the camp largely destroyed. Previously a major militant stronghold.

label: Tulkarm (West Bank)
sub: West Bank city and refugee camp subject to major IDF operations in 2024–2025. Displaced Palestinian residents; infrastructure damaged. Part of broader Israeli counter-terrorism campaign in the West Bank concurrent with Gaza war.

label: Sinwar Family Home (Khan Younis)
sub: Childhood home area of Yahya Sinwar in Khan Younis. IDF targeted the area early in the Khan Younis offensive believing senior Hamas leaders were hiding there. Symbol of the hunt for Hamas leadership.

label: Cairo (Ceasefire Negotiations)
sub: Egyptian capital and one of two primary venues for Gaza ceasefire negotiations (alongside Doha). Egypt's intelligence service served as key mediator. Multiple rounds of indirect talks between Israel and Hamas delegations held in Cairo.

label: Doha (Hamas HQ & Negotiations)
sub: Qatari capital hosting Hamas Political Bureau and primary ceasefire negotiation venue. Qatar's foreign ministry served as main interlocutor between Hamas and Israeli negotiators. Site of Phase 1 ceasefire deal finalized January 2025.

label: ICJ — The Hague
sub: Seat of the International Court of Justice where South Africa filed genocide case against Israel. ICJ issued provisional measures in January and March 2024, and ordered halt to Rafah operation in May 2024. Major legal front of the conflict.

label: Red Sea / Suez Approach
sub: Houthi attacks on shipping in the Red Sea and Bab el-Mandeb Strait forced commercial vessels to reroute. Global trade disruption; shipping costs surged. US-led Operation Prosperity Guardian conducted strikes on Houthi positions in Yemen.

label: Al-Zeitoun Quarter (Gaza City)
sub: Southeastern neighborhood of Gaza City; particularly heavily bombed. Major Hamas tunnel infrastructure discovered by IDF. Scenes of widespread destruction throughout the ground operation.

label: Al-Nasr Hospital (North Gaza)
sub: Children's hospital in northern Gaza; one of the first hospitals to close due to lack of fuel and supplies in October 2023. WHO reported neonatal patients died after life-support machines failed.

label: Wadi Gaza (Central Divide)
sub: Natural geographic barrier dividing northern and southern Gaza. IDF used the Wadi as part of its strategy to segment Gaza. Crossing points controlled and monitored by IDF during ground operations.

label: UNRWA Shelter Schools (Nuseirat)
sub: Multiple UNRWA school buildings used as shelters in Nuseirat camp. Site of June 2024 hostage rescue operation that killed 274+ civilians. Repeated strikes on UNRWA-designated facilities drew international condemnation.

label: Nahal Oz (IDF Post)
sub: Small kibbutz and IDF military base on the Gaza border. Attacked on October 7; IDF observation soldiers (mostly young women) were killed and captured. Multiple hostages taken from here.

label: Kibbutz Nir Oz
sub: Small kibbutz where approximately 25% of residents were either killed or kidnapped on October 7 — the highest proportion of any community. Some entire families were taken hostage. Community largely destroyed and evacuated.

label: Nova Memorial (Re'im)
sub: Memorial established at the site of the Nova music festival massacre. Became major gathering place for hostage families and vigils. Represents the October 7 tragedy in Israeli public consciousness.

label: UNRWA Al-Fakhoura School
sub: UNRWA school in Jabalia used as shelter for thousands of displaced persons. Struck in Israeli airstrike in January 2024, killing over 100 people. UN condemned attack; IDF said militants were using the facility.

label: Abu Yousef al-Najar Hospital (Rafah)
sub: Main hospital in Rafah. Became overwhelmed as over 1.4 million people crowded into the city. Operated far beyond capacity during the Rafah offensive. Severely damaged and evacuated during Israeli operations.

label: Gush Katif Corridor (Western Gaza)
sub: Western coastal strip of Gaza running along the former Gush Katif settlements. IDF maintained military presence along this corridor. Used as staging area for operations in Rafah and Khan Younis.

label: IDF Central Gaza Positions
sub: Central Gaza IDF positions established during Phase 2 operations in 2024. Multiple divisions rotated through operations in central Gaza targeting tunnel systems and remaining Hamas infrastructure.

label: Nablus (West Bank)
sub: West Bank city with significant Palestinian militant activity post-October 7. IDF conducted repeated operations in the old city and refugee camps. Lion's Den and other armed groups based here. Home to Joseph's Tomb, a contested holy site.

label: Ramallah (Palestinian Authority)
sub: De facto capital of the Palestinian Authority, seat of Mahmoud Abbas's government. Under increased security threats from Hamas and Israeli settler violence. PA struggling to maintain legitimacy and coordinate with international partners.

label: Beersheba (IDF Southern Command)
sub: Location of IDF Southern Command headquarters and principal command center for the Gaza ground operation. Coordinated all divisions and units operating in Gaza from this facility.

label: Hostages Square (Tel Aviv)
sub: Renamed plaza in Tel Aviv where hostage families and supporters gather every week demanding a deal. Thousands attend weekly vigils. Symbol of public pressure on Netanyahu's government to prioritize hostage return over war objectives.

label: Kerem Shalom Aid Management Zone
sub: Israeli COGAT (Coordination of Government Activities in the Territories) manages humanitarian aid at Kerem Shalom. Became focus of intense international scrutiny over aid denial allegations. US established a joint mechanism here to monitor aid.

label: Hebron / Al-Khalil (West Bank)
sub: West Bank's largest city and major center of settler-Palestinian tension. Settler violence significantly increased after October 7 with IDF providing cover. Home to the Cave of the Patriarchs / Ibrahimi Mosque — a divided holy site.

label: Rimal Quarter (Gaza City)
sub: Upscale neighborhood of Gaza City housing diplomatic offices, banks, and media centers. Heavily bombed including strikes on Al Jazeera offices. Near total destruction by end of 2023 northern Gaza campaign.

label: Hatzerim IAF Base
sub: Main Israeli Air Force base near Beersheba launching airstrikes on Gaza. F-35I Adir jets flew combat missions from this base. Home to IAF aerobatics team and major strike aircraft.

label: Kibbutz Alumim
sub: Small kibbutz near Gaza border attacked on October 7. Residents sheltered in safe rooms as militants entered the community. Several killed; community evacuated.

label: Khan Younis Police Vehicle Strike (Apr 25)
sub: Israeli airstrike on a police vehicle near a wedding hall in Khan Younis, April 25, 2026. Killed 7 including 4 police officers and 3 civilian bystanders. Gaza interior ministry condemned the strike as 'unprecedented bloody, fascist conduct.'

label: Sheikh Radwan Strike (Apr 25)
sub: Israeli strike near a bank in Gaza City's Sheikh Radwan neighborhood, April 25, 2026. Killed 3 including 2 police officers. IDF claimed Hamas militants were targeted.
